author: Besting
text: REVISED 2026-06-06: a fresh landing's substrate is SEEDED by copying the app-level default-config/ folder into balls/config (not hand-built defaults). The seed carries tracker/delivery/builtin plugin wiring, so they are ordinary landing entries — no runtime plugin defaults. (Seed mechanism itself = new sibling task; this task just creates the landing/store branches + binding and invokes the seed.) spec bl-2e26 §1/§12.

author: Aurally
text: Coordination (2026-06-06, after bl-35f7 landed c6b9569): the task-op DISPATCH that resolves the terminus + builds the §7 binding now lives in src/mutate.rs (bl-35f7, the §9 mutating-verb wiring) — it post-dates this ball's 'WHAT amends' list (bl-e755/bl-5c65/bl-be5f/bl-4450), so don't miss it. mutate::run currently does ALL of the following against the single clone.operating()/STATE_BRANCH and must be split to the two-branch model: (1) Registry::at(&operating).resolve(op,phase) — the plugin chain lives on the LANDING (config/plugins, §2), so resolve from the landing checkout; (2) the CHANGE worktree + Git::at(&operating).seal — task ops seal onto the STORE (tasks_branch), so the terminus is the store checkout; (3) it builds the binding via checkout::binding(operating, invocation, origin_of(operating), STATE_BRANCH). NOTE: bl-35f7 made checkout::binding + checkout::origin_of pub(crate) and mutate REUSES them — so the new {remote,tasks_branch,store,landing,invocation_path} binding has ONE construction point (checkout::binding); update its signature there and fix the two call sites (checkout prime/sync + mutate). So 'task ops seal onto the store' = editing mutate.rs, not just substrate/engine.
